description: Calling a transition not declared on a binding's substate fires E-STATE-TRANSITION-ILLEGAL (§54.6.3). `sub` is typed `Draft`, which declares the transition `validate()`; `sub.wrongName()` names no declared transition, so the type system reports E-STATE-TRANSITION-ILLEGAL. The canonical `< Draft body(string)>` substate syntax incidentally co-fires E-TYPE-UNKNOWN-NAME + W-WHITESPACE-001, which are left out of notCodes; W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED is ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §54.6.3 a call `binding.method()` where the binding's resolved substate declares transitions but not `method` is a compile error (E-STATE-TRANSITION-ILLEGAL), steering to a typo fix or the expected narrowed substate.
